soap:
REVIEW · lance-format/lance#7463 — feat(index): read a column's min/max from ZoneMap without a scan
Ali2Arslan (first_time) · L (+357/-2, 5 files) · no escalation

ONE-LINER
  Folds the per-zone min/max already stored in a ZoneMap scalar index into a single
  global (min, max) for a column with no data scan, exposed as
  DatasetIndexExt::column_value_range(column). Intended as a cheap planning input
  (range pruning) and stats accessor. Conservative by construction: the returned
  range is a superset of live values, safe to prune with, never tight.

SUBJECTIVE (author's framing)
  PR claims two soundness guards: (1) NaN bail — any zone whose max is NaN forces
  None, because ScalarValue total order ranks NaN above all finite values, so a
  NaN-bearing zone hides its true finite max and folding only finite maxes would
  yield an unsound *subset*; (2) joint fragment coverage — the dataset accessor
  returns None unless the column's ZoneMap segments jointly cover every live
  fragment. Multi-segment indices are folded raw (over zones, not per-segment
  value_range) to stay exact. Result is conservative under deletion vectors.

OBJECTIVE (verified against the diff at head 1fc46c07)
  • zonemap.rs:167-188 value_range_over — fold loop. Verified: bails to None on the
    FIRST zone with NaN max (line 173, before any accumulation), so a single NaN
    zone anywhere in any segment poisons the whole fold. Correct and matches the
    documented contract.
  • zonemap.rs:176-185 — min takes smallest finite zone.min, max takes largest
    finite zone.max via partial_cmp + is_some_and. Verified min-side soundness:
    each zone.min is the true min of its (post-superset) zone, so the global min =
    smallest zone min is a valid lower bound; max symmetric. partial_cmp returning
    None (incomparable) is treated as "not better" and skipped — safe, and all
    segments are over the same field so type mismatch can't arise.
  • NaN-min asymmetry checked: with NaN ranked above all finite values, max >= min
    means a NaN min implies a NaN max, which is already caught by the line-173 bail.
    No separate NaN-min guard needed. Confirmed against scalar_is_nan (zonemap.rs:134)
    and the existing zone_has_finite_min/extrema helpers (144-151).
  • scalar_is_finite_bound (zonemap.rs:192) — !is_null && !is_nan; new helper, but
    duplicates the predicate already inlined in zone_has_finite_min. Minor.
  • index.rs:1458-1515 column_value_range — segment selection filters by single
    matching field_id AND index_details.type_url ending in "ZoneMapIndexDetails",
    so a BTree on the same column is excluded. Verified coverage check: unions each
    segment's fragment_bitmap (None bitmap → None result) and requires
    self.fragment_bitmap.is_subset(covered). An appended-but-unindexed fragment is
    NOT in covered → None. Extra dead fragments in the union are harmless. Sound.
  • index.rs:1500-1514 — opened indices kept alive in `opened` so the borrowed
    ZoneMapIndex refs survive the fold; downcast-miss returns None. Correct lifetime
    handling; downcast can't realistically miss given the type_url filter, and None
    on miss is conservatively safe anyway.
  • Tests verify every critical edge: spans fragments/zones, all-null→None,
    NaN max→None, multi-segment fold, NaN-in-any-segment→None, all-null-segment
    skipped (zonemap.rs tests); dataset basic range, no-index column→None,
    appended-uncovered-fragment→None, multi-segment commit fold (scalar.rs tests).
    The two highest-risk soundness paths (NaN bail, uncovered-fragment bail) are
    each covered by a dedicated test. codecov/patch check is green.
  • CI: all required checks green at head (clippy, format, rustdoc, MSRV, codecov,
    cross-platform builds, Python/Java/integration). mergeable_state=blocked is the
    missing required human review, not a failing check. (Packet baseline showed CI
    red — stale; re-checked live.)
  • could not read: none (0 omitted files).

ASSESSMENT
  risk: low (refined down from baseline med) — additive, read-only API; no format
    read/write or wire-compat change; no unsafe/concurrency; the only correctness
    surface is the soundness of the folded bound, which is conservative-by-design
    (errs toward None) and the two unsound-subset traps are each test-covered.
    Worst plausible failure is an over-conservative None, never an unsound range.
  urgency: low — new opt-in accessor, no existing caller wired to it yet.
  escalation: none (not public-API-breaking; purely additive trait method with a
    default-None impl on ScalarIndex, so no downstream impls break).
  blast radius: contained to lance-index ZoneMap + one new dataset method.

DRAFT COMMENTS
  praise(non-blocking): The NaN bail and joint-fragment-coverage guards are the
    right two traps, and each has a dedicated test (test_value_range_nan_max_is_none,
    test_column_value_range_none_when_index_misses_fragments). The "conservative,
    superset, safe-to-prune, not tight" contract is documented at every layer.

  suggestion(non-blocking): PR description names the dataset method
    `zonemap_value_range` / `DatasetIndexExt::zonemap_value_range`, but the shipped
    method is `column_value_range` (api.rs:248, index.rs:1458). Harmless drift, but
    update the description so the docs/changelog match the actual public name.

  suggestion(non-blocking): scalar_is_finite_bound (zonemap.rs:192) re-expresses the
    same !is_null && !is_nan predicate already inlined in zone_has_finite_min
    (zonemap.rs:144). Consider expressing one in terms of the other to keep the NaN
    policy single-sourced. Author's call; not gating.

  question(non-blocking): column_value_range opens each matched segment via
    open_generic_index on every call (no cache of the folded result). Fine for a
    planning-time call; is repeated invocation on a hot path a concern, or is this
    expected to be called once per plan? Does not gate.

RECOMMENDATION: approve (comment) — no blocking conditions.
  confidence: high. The soundness argument holds under inspection (min lower-bound
  validity, NaN-min subsumed by NaN-max bail, uncovered-fragment bail), CI is green,
  and the two unsound-subset failure modes are each test-covered.
  what would make this wrong: if a ZoneMap zone.min could ever be *larger* than some
  live value in that zone (i.e. min not a true lower bound) — that would break the
  global-min soundness — but that contradicts the existing ZoneMap invariant the
  rest of the file already relies on for pruning. No evidence of that here.
